title: The random Module
subtitle: Generate random numbers, make random choices, shuffle data
learning_objectives: Pick a random item from a list with choice()
Shuffle a list in-place with shuffle()

sections.heading: Generating Random Numbers
sections.content:
The random module generates pseudo-random numbers. The most used functions are:
  • random.random() — a float between 0.0 and 1.0
  • random.randint(a, b) — a random integer between a and b (inclusive)
  • random.uniform(a, b) — a random float between a and b
sections.code_example:
import random

# Float between 0.0 and 1.0
print(random.random())

# Integer between 1 and 6 (like a dice roll)
print(random.randint(1, 6))

# Float between 1.0 and 10.0
print(random.uniform(1, 10))
sections.code_output:
0.374... (varies each run)
4 (varies each run)
7.82... (varies each run)
sections.note: Your output will be different each time you run the code — that's what 'random' means!

sections.heading: Choosing and Shuffling
sections.content: random.choice(seq) picks one random item from a list. random.choices(seq, k=n) picks n items (with replacement). random.shuffle(list) rearranges the list in-place. random.sample(seq, k) picks k unique items.
sections.code_example:
import random

fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "date"]

# Pick one
print(random.choice(fruits))

# Pick 2 (with replacement — same item can appear twice)
print(random.choices(fruits, k=2))

# Shuffle in-place
random.shuffle(fruits)
print(fruits)

# Pick 2 unique items
print(random.sample(fruits, 2))

exercises.title: Simulate a Dice Roll
exercises.prompt: Write a function roll_dice() that returns a random integer between 1 and 6. Call it 3 times and print each result.
exercises.starter_code:
import random

def roll_dice():
    # Return a random int between 1 and 6
    pass

print(roll_dice())
print(roll_dice())
print(roll_dice())
hints: Return the result from the function

exercises.title: Random Name Picker
exercises.prompt: You have a list of names: ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Carol', 'Dave', 'Eve']. Pick and print one random name. Then shuffle the list and print the first 3 names.
exercises.starter_code:
import random

names = ["Alice", "Bob", "Carol", "Dave", "Eve"]

# Print one random name

# Shuffle names and print first 3
exercises.solution:
import random

names = ["Alice", "Bob", "Carol", "Dave", "Eve"]

print(random.choice(names))

random.shuffle(names)
print(names[:3])
hints: names[:3] gets the first 3 after shuffling

quiz.question: What does random.randint(1, 10) return?
options: A random integer from 1 to 9 (not including 10)
A random integer from 1 to 10 (including both 1 and 10)
quiz.explanation: random.randint(a, b) returns a random integer where BOTH a and b are possible results. So randint(1, 10) can return any of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10.

quiz.question: What is the difference between random.choice() and random.sample()?
options: choice picks one item; sample picks multiple unique items
choice is for lists; sample is for strings
quiz.explanation: random.choice(seq) picks exactly one item. random.sample(seq, k) picks k items without repetition (unique). random.choices(seq, k=n) picks n items WITH possible repetition.

completion_message: The random module opens up a world of games, simulations, and randomised programs!
